title: Fine-Tuning LLMs
summary: Master Fine-Tuning LLMs: trade-offs, failure modes, and production patterns.
guide:
# Fine-Tuning LLMs

> **Reading time:** ~15 min

## What is Fine-Tuning LLMs?

Fine-Tuning LLMs addresses a core problem in distributed systems.
Understanding WHY it exists is more important than its API.

## Core Trade-offs

- Consistency vs availability
- Speed vs durability
- Simplicity vs power

## When to Use

Add Fine-Tuning LLMs when the problem it solves is actively costing you at your scale.

## Common Failure Modes

1. Misconfiguration (timeouts, retries)
2. Cascade failures
3. Data inconsistency on partial failure

## Mind Map

```
Fine-Tuning LLMs
├── Core abstraction
├── Configuration options
├── Failure modes
└── Monitoring metrics
```

## Connections

Related topics: fine-tuning

questions.prompt: Core concept 1: which statement best describes Fine-Tuning LLMs?
choices: Fine-Tuning LLMs always requires dedicated hardware
Fine-Tuning LLMs trade-offs depend on requirements and scale
Fine-Tuning LLMs is only for large enterprises
Fine-Tuning LLMs eliminates all database needs
questions.explanation: Fine-Tuning LLMs is context-dependent. Trade-offs matter more than API memorisation.

questions.prompt: The core trade-off in Fine-Tuning LLMs is between correctness and ___.
questions.explanation: Distributed systems involve the tension between correctness/consistency and performance/availability.

questions.prompt: When should you avoid adding Fine-Tuning LLMs to a system?
choices: When it has fewer than 1000 users
When complexity outweighs its benefits at your current scale
Never - always include it
Only for cloud deployments
questions.explanation: Add complexity only when the problem it solves is actively costing you.

questions.prompt: Which metric best indicates a Fine-Tuning LLMs system is healthy?
choices: CPU usage
Error rate and p99 latency
Disk space
Log file size
questions.explanation: Error rate + p99 latency are leading indicators of customer impact.

flashcards.back: Consistency vs availability, or speed vs durability. Where does this topic stand?
When the problem it solves is actively costing you at your scale.

flashcards.front: When NOT to use Fine-Tuning LLMs?
flashcards.back: When simpler alternatives work. Complexity has a maintenance cost.

flashcards.front: Top 3 failure modes of Fine-Tuning LLMs?
flashcards.back: 1. Misconfiguration. 2. Cascade failure. 3. Data inconsistency on partial failure.

flashcards.front: How to monitor Fine-Tuning LLMs in production?
flashcards.back: Error rate, p99 latency, throughput, saturation (queue depth / connections).

flashcards.front: Fine-Tuning LLMs in one interview sentence?
flashcards.back: [Draft: what it is + what problem it solves + when to use it]

flashcards.front: How does Fine-Tuning LLMs connect to caching?
flashcards.back: Most systems cache upstream of expensive operations. Cache-aside is the default pattern.

project.brief:
## Project: Design a System Using Fine-Tuning LLMs

Design a realistic production service with Fine-Tuning LLMs at its core. No code required:

1. Why Fine-Tuning LLMs over a simpler alternative?
2. Key configuration decisions and reasoning
3. Failure scenarios and recovery mechanisms
4. Monitoring: metrics and alert thresholds
5. How does it scale to 10x load?

checklist.text: Justified use of Fine-Tuning LLMs for a specific use case
Named 3+ key configuration decisions with justifications
Identified 2+ failure scenarios with recovery mechanisms
Specified 3+ monitoring metrics with example thresholds
Described scaling approach and first bottleneck

hints: Start with the problem. What pain does Fine-Tuning LLMs remove?
Configuration: think timeouts, retries, consistency levels, batch sizes.
Monitoring: error_rate, p50/p99 latency, queue_depth, saturation are starting points.
